findings.description: subprocess.run with shell=True interpolates attacker-controlled src/dest from job payload, enabling arbitrary shell command injection.
attempt=0 reset inside the loop before the increment keeps attempt at 0 at the top of each iteration, so a persistently-failing job retries forever (infinite loop).
Backoff uses (attempt+1) right after attempt was reset to 0, so sleep is always a constant 2.0s and never exponential despite the BACKOFF naming.
async poll_loop calls blocking time.sleep() instead of await asyncio.sleep(), blocking the entire event loop and starving all other coroutines.
poll_loop has no await/yield point, so it cannot be cleanly cancelled via asyncio cancellation and spins forever when stop_after is None and the queue stays empty.
list.pop(0) is O(n) per dequeue (O(n^2) to drain); a collections.deque should back the queue.
JobQueue push/pop are not thread-safe or coroutine-safe; concurrent async poll_loop plus sync drain_sync can lose or duplicate jobs with no locking.
Bare except Exception swallows all errors including programming bugs and, combined with the infinite-retry loop, retries forever on any raised exception.
sum(values) assumes numeric values with no validation; a payload with strings or None raises TypeError that then feeds the infinite-retry loop.
src/dest default to empty strings, so a missing path silently runs 'convert  ' instead of failing fast on misconfiguration.
stop_after is only checked after processing a job, so stop_after=0 still executes one job (off-by-one against the requested limit).
asyncio is imported but never used (the async loop relies on time.sleep), dead import signalling the async/sync mismatch.
